scenario.context: Station 7 of the daily run: test network functionality, maintain, and track configuration changes.
scenario.mission: You plan quick tests, preventive maintenance, and clean documentation of configuration changes.

station.profile: Connectivity testing, maintenance, configuration change, and monitoring
constraints: Focus tests on the foundation first

customerRequest.subject: Station 07: prepare a maintenance and testing flow for client networks
customerRequest.body: Please develop a compact testing and maintenance flow for the client network. Important are baseline diagnosis, orderly configuration changes, and a short maintenance note.

questions.title: First step in a connectivity test
questions.prompt: What is the most sensible first step when a connectivity issue is reported?

options.text: Check physical connection and link status
options.rationale: This secures the technical foundation first.

options.text: Replace the entire device immediately without analysis
options.rationale: That is not economical without evidence.
That does not help initial diagnosis.

feedback.correct: Correct: Diagnosis starts with the base connection.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: check the physical foundation first.

questions.title: Preventive maintenance in the client network
questions.prompt: Which measures belong to sensible preventive maintenance? (Multiple selections)

options.rationale: Pattern recognition is the basis for sustainable improvements.

options.text: Plan firmware or configuration changes in maintenance windows
Back up configuration states before changes
After changes, check connectivity and name resolution
options.rationale: This directly verifies the effect of the change.

feedback.correct: Correct: You link maintenance, rollback options, and effectiveness checks.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: emphasize prevention, backups, and post-tests more.

questions.title: Change to a network configuration
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean configuration change.
items: Approve the change in the ticket or task
Back up the previous configuration state
Document the result and new state
correctOrder: Approve the change in the ticket or task
Back up the previous configuration state
Document the result and new state

feedback.correct: Correct: This keeps the change controlled and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: think approval, backup, change, test, and documentation in that order.

questions.title: Planned maintenance time
questions.prompt: Three maintenance windows per month each last 45 minutes. How many minutes of maintenance time are planned in total?

feedback.wrong: Multiply the number of maintenance windows by the duration.

questions.title: Write a maintenance note
questions.prompt: Write in 4-6 sentences a maintenance note with performed configuration, test result, and next date.
questions.expectedOutline: Briefly and traceably record the change, test status, and follow-up date.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is useful for operations and follow-up.
feedback.wrong: Please separate maintenance, configuration change, and follow-up date more clearly.
